songs.title: willow
songs.lyrics:
I'm like the water when your ship rolled in that night
Rough on the surface, but you cut through like a knife
And if it was an open-shut case
I never would've known from that look on your face
Lost in your current like a priceless wine

The more that you say, the less I know
Wherever you stray, I follow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man

Life was a willow and it bent right to your wind
Head on the pillow, I could feel you sneakin' in
As if you were a mythical thing
Like you were a trophy or a champion ring
And there was one prize I'd cheat to win

The more that you say, the less I know
Wherever you stray, I follow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man
You know that my train could take you home
Anywhere else is hollow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man

Life was a willow and it bent right to your wind
They count me out time and time again
Life was a willow and it bent right to your wind
But I come back stronger than a '90s trend

Wait for the signal, and I'll meet you after dark
Show me the places where the others gave you scars
Now this is an open-shut case
I guess I should've known from the look on your face
Every bait-and-switch was a work of art

The more that you say, the less I know
Wherever you stray, I follow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man
You know that my train could take you home
Anywhere else is hollow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man
The more that you say, the less I know
Wherever you stray, I follow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man
You know that my train could take you home
Anywhere else is hollow
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man

Hey, that's my man
That's my man
Yeah, that's my man
Every bait-and-switch was a work of art
That's my man
Hey, that's my man
I'm begging for you to take my hand
Wreck my plans, that's my man

vocab.definition: Existing only in myth or legend, not based on fact. Something that is mythical is often considered extraordinary or imaginary.
vocab.lyric_snippet: As if you were a mythical thing
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'mythical' to describe the person she's addressing, implying they seem almost unreal or legendary to her. This adds to the enchanting and captivating atmosphere of the song.

vocab.definition: A deceptive tactic where something is offered or presented in a certain way, but then switched with something else, often of lesser value or quality. This phrase is often used to describe a form of deception or trickery.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Every bait-and-switch was a work of art
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bait-and-switch' to suggest that the person she's addressing has a tendency to deceive or manipulate others, but in a charming or skillful way.

vocab.definition: Having a empty or vacant space inside, often used to describe something that lacks substance or feeling. Something that is hollow can also be insincere or lacking in genuineness.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Anywhere else is hollow
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'hollow' to describe how other places or options feel empty or unfulfilling compared to being with the person she loves.

vocab.definition: A case or situation that is very clear or obvious, often used to describe something that is straightforward or easy to understand.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Now this is an open-shut case
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'open-shut' to describe a situation that is clear or obvious, but also implies that she should have known or seen it coming.

songs.title: champagne problems
songs.lyrics:
You booked the night train for a reason
So you could sit there in this hurt
Bustling crowds or silent sleepers
You're not sure which is worse

Because I dropped your hand while dancing
Left you out there standing
Crestfallen on the landing
Champagne problems
Your mom's ring in your pocket
My picture in your wallet
Your heart was glass, I dropped it
Champagne problems

You told your family for a reason
You couldn't keep it in
Your sister splashed out on the bottle
Now no one's celebrating

Dom Pérignon, you brought it
No crowd of friends applauded
Your hometown skeptics called it
Champagne problems
You had a speech, you're speechless
Love slipped beyond your reaches
And I couldn't give a reason
Champagne problems

Your Midas touch on the Chevy door
November flush and your flannel cure
"This dorm was once a madhouse"
I made a joke, "Well, it's made for me"
How evergreen, our group of friends
Don't think we'll say that word again
And soon they'll have the nerve to deck the halls
That we once walked through
One for the money, two for the show
I never was ready so I watch you go
Sometimes you just don't know the answer
'Til someone's on their knees and asks you
"She would've made such a lovely bride
What a shame she's fucked in the head," they said
But you'll find the real thing instead
She'll patch up your tapestry that I shred

And hold your hand while dancing
Never leave you standing
Crestfallen on the landing
With champagne problems
Your mom's ring in your pocket
Her picture in your wallet
You won't remember all my
Champagne problems

You won't remember all my
Champagne problems

vocab.definition: feeling suddenly and deeply disappointed or disheartened
vocab.lyric_snippet: Crestfallen on the landing
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the emotional state of the person who was left standing after being dropped while dancing, emphasizing their deep disappointment and heartache

vocab.definition: relating to or resembling the legendary king Midas, who was said to have the power to turn everything he touched into gold
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your Midas touch on the Chevy door
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's touch, implying that they have a magical or special effect on things, in this case, the Chevy door

vocab.definition: remaining fresh and interesting, or always relevant
vocab.lyric_snippet: How evergreen, our group of friends
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the group of friends, suggesting that their friendship remains fresh and enduring despite the challenges they face

vocab.definition: a complex or intricate situation, or a decorative piece of fabric with intricate designs
vocab.lyric_snippet: She'll patch up your tapestry that I shred
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's emotional or psychological state, implying that it has been damaged or torn apart, and that someone else will help repair it

vocab.definition: people who are doubtful or questioning, often in a critical or disbelieving way
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your hometown skeptics called it
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the people from the person's hometown who are critical or disbelieving of their relationship or situation

songs.title: gold rush
songs.lyrics:
Gleaming, twinkling
Eyes like sinking
Ships on waters
So inviting, I almost jump in

But I don't like a gold rush, gold rush
I don't like anticipatin' my face in a red flush
I don't like that anyone would die to feel your touch
Everybody wants you
Everybody wonders what it would be like to love you
Walk past, quick brush
I don't like slow motion, double vision in rose blush
I don't like that falling feels like flying 'til the bone crush
Everybody wants you
But I don't like a gold rush

What must it be like to grow up that beautiful?
With your hair falling into place like dominoes
I see me padding across your wooden floors
With my Eagles T-shirt hanging from the door

At dinner parties, I call you out on your contrarian shit
And the coastal town we wandered 'round had nеver seen a love as pure as it
And thеn it fades into the gray of my day-old tea
'Cause it could never be

'Cause I don't like a gold rush, gold rush
I don't like anticipatin' my face in a red flush
I don't like that anyone would die to feel your touch
Everybody wants you
Everybody wonders what it would be like to love you
Walk past, quick brush
I don't like slow motion, double vision in rose blush
I don't like that falling feels like flying 'til the bone crush
Everybody wants you
But I don't like a gold rush

What must it be like to grow up that beautiful?
With your hair falling into place like dominoes
My mind turns your life into folklore
I can't dare to dream about you anymore

At dinner parties, I won't call you out on your contrarian shit
And the coastal town we never found will never see a love as pure as it
'Cause it fades into the gray of my day-old tea
'Cause it will never be

Gleaming, twinkling
Eyes like sinking
Ships on waters
So inviting, I almost jump in

vocab.definition: A person who takes an opposing view, especially for the sake of being difficult or provocative. This term refers to someone who often disagrees with others, not necessarily because they believe in their own viewpoint, but to challenge the status quo.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I call you out on your contrarian shit
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe someone who often takes an opposing view, possibly to be provocative or challenging. In the song, she calls out this behavior at dinner parties, indicating a level of intellectual or philosophical debate in their interactions.

vocab.definition: Small, flat, rectangular blocks with numbers or dots on them, used for playing various games. In a broader sense, dominoes can also refer to a situation where one event triggers a series of similar events, like a row of falling dominoes.
vocab.lyric_snippet: your hair falling into place like dominoes
vocab.context: Taylor uses dominoes as a metaphor to describe how the person's hair falls perfectly into place, suggesting a natural, effortless beauty that triggers a chain reaction of admiration or envy.

vocab.definition: The traditional stories, customs, and superstitions of a particular community or culture, passed down through the generations by word of mouth. In modern usage, folklore can also refer to a body of stories or legends about a particular subject or person.
vocab.lyric_snippet: My mind turns your life into folklore
vocab.context: Taylor uses folklore to describe how her mind romanticizes or mythologizes the person's life, turning it into a kind of legend or story that is larger than life.

vocab.definition: Shining brightly and smoothly, often in a way that is attractive or impressive. This term can be used to describe something that is polished, reflective, or radiant.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Gleaming, twinkling
vocab.context: Taylor uses gleaming to describe the person's eyes, emphasizing their brightness and attractiveness, setting the tone for the rest of the song.

vocab.definition: Attractive or appealing, making someone want to participate, join, or engage with something. This term can be used to describe a situation, a place, or even a person that is welcoming or enticing.
vocab.lyric_snippet: So inviting, I almost jump in
vocab.context: Taylor uses inviting to describe the allure of the person's eyes, which are so attractive that she feels drawn to them, almost irresistibly.

vocab.definition: Shining with a faint, sparkling light, often in a way that is attractive or playful. This term can be used to describe something that is lively, animated, or full of energy.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Gleaming, twinkling
vocab.context: Taylor uses twinkling to add to the vivid description of the person's eyes, emphasizing their sparkle and liveliness.

songs.title: 'tis the damn season
songs.lyrics:
If I wanted to know who you were hanging with
While I was gone, I would've asked you
It's the kind of cold, fogs up windshield glass
But I felt it when I passed you
There's an ache in you, put there by the ache in me
But if it's all the same to you
It's the same to me

So we could call it even
You could call me "babe" for the weekend
​'Tis the damn season, write this down
I'm stayin' at my parents' house
And the road not taken looks real good now
And it always leads to you and my hometown

I parkеd my car right between the Methodist
And thе school that used to be ours
The holidays linger like bad perfume
You can run, but only so far
I escaped it too, remember how you watched me leave
But if it's okay with you, it's okay with me

We could call it even
You could call me "babe" for the weekend
​'Tis the damn season, write this down
I'm stayin' at my parents' house
And the road not taken looks real good now
Time flies, messy as the mud on your truck tires
Now I'm missing your smile, hear me out
We could just ride around
And the road not taken looks real good now
And it always leads to you and my hometown

Sleep in half the day just for old times' sake
I won't ask you to wait if you don't ask me to stay
So I'll go back to L.A. and the so-called friends
Who'll write books about me if I ever make it
And wonder about the only soul
Who can tell which smiles I'm fakin'
And the heart I know I'm breakin' is my own
To leave the warmest bed I've ever known
We could call it even
Even though I'm leaving
And I'll be yours for the weekend
​'Tis the damn season

We could call it even
You could call me "babe" for the weekend
​'Tis the damn season, write this down
I'm stayin' at my parents' house
And the road not taken looks real good now
Time flies, messy as the mud on your truck tires
Now I'm missing your smile, hear me out
We could just ride around
And the road not taken looks real good now
And it always leads to you and my hometown

It always leads to you and my hometown

vocab.definition: To remain or stay in a place for a long time, often in a way that is unwanted or uncomfortable. It can also refer to a feeling or memory that persists.
vocab.lyric_snippet: The holidays linger like bad perfume
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'linger' to describe how the holidays and their memories persist, much like an unwanted smell. This creates a sense of discomfort and nostalgia.

vocab.definition: Used to express that something is not genuinely or truly what it is claimed to be. It can imply a sense of doubt or skepticism.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the so-called friends
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'so-called' to express her skepticism about the authenticity of her friendships in L.A., implying that they may not be as genuine as they seem.

vocab.definition: A casual or informal way of saying 'faking', which means to pretend or simulate something, often in a way that is insincere or deceptive.
vocab.lyric_snippet: which smiles I'm fakin'
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'fakin'' to describe how she pretends to be happy or smile, even when she's not genuinely feeling that way. This highlights her ability to put on a facade.

songs.title: tolerate it
songs.lyrics:
I sit and watch you readin' with your head low
I wake and watch you breathin' with your eyes closed
I sit and watch you
I notice everything you do or don't do
You're so much older and wiser, and I

I wait by the door like I'm just a kid
Use my best colors for your portrait
Lay the table with the fancy shit
And watch you tolerate it
If it's all in my head, tell me now
Tell me I've got it wrong somehow
I know my love should be celebrated
But you tolerate it

I greet you with a battle hero's welcome
I take your indiscretions all in good fun
I sit and listеn
I polish plates until they gleam and glistеn
You're so much older and wiser, and I

I wait by the door like I'm just a kid
Use my best colors for your portrait
Lay the table with the fancy shit
And watch you tolerate it
If it's all in my head, tell me now
Tell me I've got it wrong somehow
I know my love should be celebrated
But you tolerate it

While you were out buildin' other worlds, where was I?
Where's that man who'd throw blankets over my barbed wire?
I made you my temple, my mural, my sky
Now I'm beggin' for footnotes in the story of your life
Drawin' hearts in the byline
Always takin' up too much space or time
You assume I'm fine, but what would you do if I (I)

Break free and leave us in ruins?
Took this dagger in me and removed it
Gain the weight of you, then lose it
Believe me, I could do it
If it's all in my head, tell me now
Tell me I've got it wrong somehow
I know my love should be celebrated
But you tolerate it

I sit and watch you

vocab.definition: Actions or behaviors that are considered improper or unwise, often in a social or moral context. Indiscretions can have negative consequences and may damage one's reputation.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I take your indiscretions all in good fun
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the actions of the person she loves, implying that she is willing to overlook or forgive their mistakes. This shows her devotion and willingness to accept them despite their flaws.

vocab.definition: To shine or sparkle with a soft, subtle light, often due to moisture or reflection. Glisten can also imply a sense of attractiveness or allure.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I polish plates until they gleam and glisten
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the effort she puts into making things perfect for the person she loves, highlighting her attention to detail and desire to create a pleasing atmosphere.

vocab.definition: A line or credit at the beginning of a newspaper or magazine article, typically including the author's name. In a broader sense, a byline can refer to a person's reputation or legacy.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Drawin' hearts in the byline
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express her desire to be a significant part of the person's life, to be acknowledged and remembered as an important aspect of their story.

vocab.definition: Having or covered with barbs, which are sharp points or projections, often used to describe something that is hurtful or unpleasant. Barbed can also imply a sense of danger or threat.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Where's that man who'd throw blankets over my barbed wire?
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe her emotional state, implying that she has been hurt or wounded in some way and is seeking protection or comfort from the person she loves.

vocab.definition: Notes or comments added to the bottom of a page, often used to provide additional information or clarification. In a broader sense, footnotes can refer to minor or secondary aspects of a larger story or narrative.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Now I'm beggin' for footnotes in the story of your life
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express her desire to be a significant part of the person's life, to be more than just a minor or secondary aspect of their story.

songs.title: no body, no crime
songs.lyrics:
He did it
He did it

Este's a friend of mine
We meet up every Tuesday night for dinner and a glass of wine
Este's been losin' sleep
Her husband's actin' different and it smells like infidelity
She says, "That ain't my Merlot on his mouth
That ain't my jewelry on our joint account"
No, there ain't no doubt
I think I'm gonna call him out

She says, "I think he did it, but I just can't prove it"
I think he did it, but I just can't prove it
I think he did it, but I just can't prove it
No, no body, no crime
But I ain't lettin' up until the day I die

No, no
I think he did it
No, no
He did it

Este wasn't there Tuesday night at Olive Gardеn
At her job or anywhere
Hе reports his missing wife
And I noticed when I passed his house
His truck has got some brand new tires
And his mistress moved in
Sleeps in Este's bed and everything
No, there ain't no doubt
Somebody's gotta catch him out 'cause

I think he did it, but I just can't prove it 
(He did it)
I think he did it, but I just can't prove it 
(He did it)
I think he did it, but I just can't prove it
No, no body, no crime
But I ain't lettin' up until the day I die

No, no
I think he did it
No, no
He did it

Good thing my daddy made me get a boating license when I was fifteen
And I've cleaned enough houses to know how to cover up a scene
Good thing Este's sister's gonna swear she was with me (
She was with me, dude
)
Good thing his mistress took out a big life insurance policy

They think she did it, but they just can't prove it
They think she did it, but they just can't prove it
She thinks I did it, but she just can't prove it

No, no body, no crime
I wasn't lettin' up until the day he
No, no body, no crime
I wasn't lettin' up until the day he
No, no body, no crime
I wasn't lettin' up until the day he died

vocab.definition: The act of being unfaithful to a spouse or partner, often by engaging in romantic or sexual relationships with someone else. It can also refer to a breach of trust or loyalty in other contexts.
vocab.lyric_snippet: it smells like infidelity
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the suspicion of Este's husband cheating on her, setting the tone for the rest of the song's narrative. The word implies a sense of betrayal and deceit.

vocab.definition: Shared by or belonging to two or more people, often in a business or financial context. In this case, it refers to a shared bank account.
vocab.lyric_snippet: our joint account
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the shared bank account of Este and her husband, highlighting the financial aspect of their relationship and the potential for suspicious transactions.

vocab.definition: A woman who is having a romantic or sexual relationship with a man, often in secret and without being married to him. The term can also imply a sense of scandal or impropriety.
vocab.lyric_snippet: his mistress moved in
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the woman who is having an affair with Este's husband, emphasizing the illicit nature of their relationship and the consequences that follow.

songs.title: happiness
songs.lyrics:
Honey, when I'm above the trees
I see this for what it is
But now I'm right down in it, all the years I've given
Is just shit we're dividin' up
Showed you all of my hiding spots
I was dancing when the music stopped
And in the disbelief, I can't face reinvention
I haven't met the new me yet

There'll be happiness after you
But there was happiness because of you
Both of these things can be true
There is happiness

Past the blood and bruise
Past the curses and cries
Beyond the terror in the nightfall
Haunted by the look in my eyes
That would've loved you for a lifetime
Leave it all behind
And there is happiness

Tell me, when did your winning smile
Begin to look like a smirk?
When did all our lessons start to look like weapons
Pointed at my deepest hurt?
I hope she'll be a beautiful fool
Who takes my spot next to you
No, I didn't mean that
Sorry, I can't see facts through all of my fury
You haven't met the new me yet

There'll be happiness after me
But there was happiness because of me
Both of these things, I believe
There is happiness

In our history, across our great divide
There is a glorious sunrise
Dappled with the flickers of light
From the dress I wore at midnight, leave it all behind
And there is happiness

I can't make it go away by making you a villain
I guess it's the price I paid for seven years in Heaven
And I pulled your body into mine
Every goddamn night, now I get fake niceties
No one teaches you what to do
When a good man hurts you
And you know you hurt him too

Honey, when I'm above the trees
I see it for what it is
But now my eyes leak acid rain on the pillow where you used to lay your head
After giving you the best I had
Tell me what to give after that
All you want from me now is the green light of forgiveness
You haven't met the new me yet
And I think she'll give you that

There'll be happiness after you
But there was happiness because of you too
Both of these things can be true
There is happiness

In our history, across our great divide
There is a glorious sunrise
Dappled with the flickers of light
From the dress I wore at midnight, leave it all behind
Oh, leave it all behind
Leave it all behind
And there is happiness

vocab.definition: The act of changing or recreating something, especially oneself, in a new or different way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: in the disbelief, I can't face reinvention
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to express her struggle to come to terms with the end of a relationship and the need to recreate herself.

vocab.definition: Marked with small, rounded patches of color or light, often in a random or irregular pattern.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Dappled with the flickers of light
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the warm and gentle light of a sunrise, symbolizing hope and new beginnings.

vocab.definition: Having or deserving great praise or glory; magnificent or resplendent.
vocab.lyric_snippet: There is a glorious sunrise
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the beauty and promise of a new dawn, representing a brighter future.

vocab.definition: Small, unsteady flames or flashes of light; a faint or brief appearance of something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Dappled with the flickers of light
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the gentle, dancing light of a sunrise, symbolizing the fragility and beauty of hope.

vocab.definition: A facial expression characterized by a slight, often mocking or scornful smile.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Begin to look like a smirk
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the change in her partner's smile, from genuine to mocking or condescending.

vocab.definition: Polite or courteous behavior, often insincere or superficial.
vocab.lyric_snippet: now I get fake niceties
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the shallow or pretended kindness she receives from her partner, highlighting the end of genuine affection.

songs.title: dorothea
songs.lyrics:
Hey, Dorothea, do you ever stop and think about me?
When we were younger, down in the park
Honey, makin' a lark of the misery
You got shiny friends since you left town
A tiny screen's the only place I see you now
And I got nothin' but well wishes for ya

Ooh
This place is the same as it ever was
Ooh
But you won't like it that way

It's never too late to come back to my side
The stars in your eyes shined brighter in Tupelo
And if you're ever tired of bеin' known for who you know
You know that you'll always know me
Dorothea, uh-uh
Dorothea, ah-ah

Ooh
You'rе a queen sellin' dreams, sellin' makeup and magazines
Ooh
From you, I'd buy anything

Hey, Dorothea, do you ever stop and think about me?
When it was calmer, skippin' the prom
Just to piss off your mom and her pageant schemes
And damn, Dorothea, they all wanna be ya
But are you still the same soul I met under the bleachers? Well

Ooh
I guess I'll never know
Ooh
And you'll go on with the show

But it's never too late to come back to my side
The stars in your eyes shined brighter in Tupelo
And if you're ever tired of bein' known for who you know
You know, you'll always know me
Dorothea, uh-uh
Dorothea, ah-ah

Ooh, ooh
Ooh-woo-ooh-ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh-ooh-ooh
Ooh, ooh
Ooh-woo-ooh-ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh-ooh
Dorothea (Ah-ah-ah)
Ah-ah
Ooh

vocab.definition: a carefree or playful adventure, often involving a prank or mischief. It can also refer to a type of bird known for its melodious song.
vocab.lyric_snippet: makin' a lark of the misery
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'lark' to describe how she and Dorothea used to make light of their difficult situations, turning them into playful adventures. This word highlights their youthful and carefree spirit.

vocab.definition: a show or spectacle, often with elaborate costumes and performances, or a beauty contest where participants are judged on their physical appearance.
vocab.lyric_snippet: your mom and her pageant schemes
vocab.context: Taylor mentions 'pageant' to refer to Dorothea's mother's involvement in beauty contests or similar events, implying a sense of superficiality or competition. This word adds to the narrative of Dorothea's complicated relationship with her mother.

vocab.definition: raised, tiered seats, often found in sports stadiums or arenas, where spectators can watch events.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the same soul I met under the bleachers
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bleachers' to recall a specific moment when she met Dorothea, likely at a school event or sports game. This word serves as a nostalgic reference point in their relationship.

vocab.definition: a type of tree, or a city in Mississippi, USA, known for its musical heritage, particularly in the genres of blues and rock 'n' roll.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the stars in your eyes shined brighter in Tupelo
vocab.context: Taylor mentions 'Tupelo' as a place where Dorothea's eyes sparkled, possibly referencing the city's musical significance or a personal experience they shared there. This word adds a sense of geographic and cultural depth to the narrative.

songs.title: coney island
songs.lyrics:
Break my soul in two looking for you
But you're right here
If I can't relate to you anymore
Then who am I related to?
And if this is the long haul
How'd we get here so soon?
Did I close my fist around something delicate?
Did I shatter you?

And I'm sitting on a bench in Coney Island
Wondering, where did my baby go?
The fast times, the bright lights, the merry go
Sorry for not making you my centerfold
Over and over
Lost again with no surprises
Disappointments, close your eyes
And it gets colder and colder
When the sun goes down

The question pounds my head
What's a lifetime of achievement
If I pushed you to the edge
But you were too polite to leave me?
And do you miss the rogue

Sorry for not making you my, making you my
Making you my centerfold

vocab.definition: Requiring careful handling or treatment because of fragility or sensitivity.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Did I close my fist around something delicate?
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'delicate' to describe the vulnerability of her partner, suggesting that she may have unintentionally harmed them.

vocab.definition: A person, especially a model, whose photograph is featured on the center pages of a magazine.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sorry for not making you my centerfold
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'centerfold' metaphorically to express regret for not prioritizing or idealizing her partner.

vocab.definition: Giving, expressing, or characterized by joy and laughter.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the bright lights, the merry go
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'merry' to evoke a sense of nostalgia and carefree joy associated with her memories of Coney Island.

vocab.definition: A place or state of supreme bliss or happiness.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Who coaxed you into paradise and left you there?
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'paradise' to describe the idealized state of their relationship, which she may have failed to maintain.

vocab.definition: A person who behaves in a way that is not normal or expected, often in a charming or attractive way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: And do you miss the rogue
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'rogue' to describe her past self, implying that she was once charming and alluring, but may have lost that appeal.

vocab.definition: Having or showing good manners, tact, and courtesy.
vocab.lyric_snippet: But you were too polite to leave me?
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'polite' to suggest that her partner stayed in the relationship out of courtesy or obligation, rather than genuine affection.

songs.title: ivy
songs.lyrics:
How's one to know?
I'd meet you where the spirit meets the bones
In a faith-forgotten land
In from the snow
Your touch brought forth an incandescent glow
Tarnished but so grand

And the old widow goes to the stone every day
But I don't, I just sit here and wait
Grieving for the living

Oh, goddamn
My pain fits in the palm of your freezing hand
Taking mine, but it's been promised to another
Oh, I can't
Stop you putting roots in my dreamland

The fatal flaw that makes you long to be
Magnificently cursed

He wants what's only yours

Oh, goddamn
My pain fits in the palm of your freezing hand
Taking mine, but it's been promised to another

Stop you putting roots in my dreamland
My house of stone, your ivy grows

He's gonna burn this house to the ground
How's one to know?
I'd live and die for moments that we stole
On begged and borrowed time
So tell me to run
Or dare to sit and watch what we'll become
And drink my husband's wine

Oh, goddamn
My pain fits in the palm of your freezing hand
Taking mine, but it's been promised to another
Oh, I can't
Stop you putting roots in my dreamland
My house of stone, your ivy grows
And now I'm covered in you
And I'm covered in you

So yeah, it's a fire
It's a goddamn blaze in the dark
And you started it
You started it
So yeah, it's a war

And you started it
You started it

Oh, I can't
Stop you putting roots in my dreamland
My house of stone, your ivy grows
And now I'm covered
In you, in you
Now I'm covered in you
In you

vocab.definition: Giving off light, typically in the form of rays or a glow. Incandescent objects or materials emit light as a result of being heated.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your touch brought forth an incandescent glow
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the intense, radiant effect of the person's touch. The word suggests a strong, passionate connection between the two individuals.

vocab.definition: Damaged or discredited, often in a moral or reputational sense. Something that is tarnished has lost its original shine or luster.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Tarnished but so grand
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe something that has been damaged or discredited, but still retains a sense of grandeur or beauty.

vocab.definition: In a magnificent or impressive manner. Something that is magnificent is grand, beautiful, or impressive.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Magnificently cursed
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way someone is cursed, suggesting a sense of awe or wonder at the intensity of the curse.

vocab.definition: A type of gemstone that is known for its iridescent, shimmering appearance. Opals are often associated with beauty and rarity.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Your opal eyes are all I wish to see
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the person's eyes, suggesting a sense of beauty and rarity. The comparison to an opal gemstone adds a sense of luxury and wonder to the description.

vocab.definition: Shaped like a crescent moon, or increasing in size or extent. A crescent shape is curved and growing, like the moon in its early phases.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Crescent moon, coast is clear
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the moon, suggesting a sense of growth and increase. The image of the crescent moon also adds a sense of calm and clarity to the scene.

vocab.definition: Asked or requested something in a humble or urgent manner. To beg for something is to ask for it in a way that is pleading or insistent.
vocab.lyric_snippet: On begged and borrowed time
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the way time is being used, suggesting a sense of urgency or desperation. The phrase 'begged and borrowed time' implies that the time is being used in a way that is not entirely legitimate or sustainable.

songs.title: cowboy like me

With some tent-like thing
And you asked me to dance
But I said, "Dancin' is a dangerous game"
Oh, I thought
This is gonna be one of those things
Now I know
I'm never gonna love again

Forever is the sweetest con

I've had some tricks up my sleeve
Takes one to know one
You're a cowboy like me

And I'm never gonna love again
I'm never gonna love again
I'm never gonna love again

vocab.definition: sitting or resting in a high or elevated position, often in a temporary or precarious way. It can also imply a sense of being poised or ready to act.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Perched in the dark
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'perched' to describe the narrator's position, suggesting a sense of being poised and ready to take advantage of a situation, while also implying a sense of vulnerability.

vocab.definition: a person who takes things that do not belong to them, often in a sneaky or dishonest way. It can also refer to someone who is seen as charmingly dishonest or unscrupulous.
vocab.lyric_snippet: You're a bandit like me
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'bandit' to describe the narrator and the person they're addressing, implying a sense of shared dishonesty or unscrupulousness.

vocab.definition: working hard to achieve something, often in a way that is considered aggressive or unscrupulous. It can also imply a sense of striving for success or wealth.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Hustling for the good life
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'hustling' to describe the narrator's efforts to achieve a better life, suggesting a sense of determination and ambition, but also potentially implying a sense of desperation or unscrupulousness.

vocab.definition: to cheat or deceive someone in order to get their money or property. It can also imply a sense of being taken advantage of or exploited.
vocab.lyric_snippet: the old men that I've swindled
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'swindled' to describe the narrator's past actions, implying a sense of dishonesty or exploitation.

vocab.definition: a trick or a scam, often used to deceive or cheat someone. It can also refer to a confidence trick or a swindle.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Forever is the sweetest con
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'con' to describe the idea of forever or long-term commitment, implying that it is a kind of trick or illusion that is appealing but potentially deceptive.

vocab.definition: In a way that seems to be controlled by fate; inevitably. Fatefully describes events that are predetermined or destined to happen.
vocab.lyric_snippet: fatefully i tried to pick
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'fatefully' to describe how she tried to pick her battles but ultimately ended up being chosen by the battle itself, implying a sense of inevitability.

vocab.definition: A steep cliff or a situation of great danger or uncertainty. A precipice is a point of great risk or a critical moment.
vocab.lyric_snippet: pushed from the precipice
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'precipice' to describe being pushed to the edge of a dangerous or uncertain situation, emphasizing the risk and vulnerability she felt.

vocab.definition: A person or thing that is a source of great trouble or opposition. A nemesis is an archenemy or a long-standing rival.
vocab.lyric_snippet: your nemeses will defeat
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'nemeses' to advise someone not to get lost in petty things and to trust that their enemies will ultimately defeat themselves.

vocab.definition: A base or support for a statue or a position of great respect or admiration. To be on a pedestal means to be highly regarded or idealized.
vocab.lyric_snippet: and i fell from the pedestal
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'pedestal' to describe how she fell from a position of great respect or admiration, implying a loss of status or reputation.

vocab.definition: A faint or fleeting sparkle of light. A glimmer can also be a small sign or indication of something.
vocab.lyric_snippet: rare as the glimmer
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'glimmer' to describe the rare and special feeling of being with someone who feels like home, emphasizing the uniqueness of the experience.

vocab.definition: Having or showing good manners, respectful and considerate of others.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to caution against prioritizing politeness over personal power, highlighting the importance of balance in relationships.

vocab.definition: A yellowish-orange color, or a type of fossilized tree resin.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the color of the sky, evoking a sense of warmth and nostalgia in the song's narrative.

vocab.definition: Accumulated or delayed, often due to a lack of resources or time.

vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the accumulation of unfulfilled dreams and aspirations, highlighting the idea that Marjorie's legacy lives on through her.

vocab.definition: To hold or use something, especially a weapon or a power, in a skillful or effective way.
vocab.lyric_snippet: Never wield such power
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to caution against misusing power, emphasizing the importance of balance and responsibility in relationships.

vocab.definition: The state of being able to bring a situation or experience to an end, often providing a sense of resolution or finality.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I don't need your closure
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'closure' to express her independence from her former partner's attempts to reconcile or provide a sense of finality to their relationship.

vocab.definition: A small problem or difficulty that needs to be dealt with, often used figuratively to describe a minor issue or complication.
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm just a wrinkle in your new life
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'wrinkle' to describe herself as a minor issue in her former partner's new life, implying that she is no longer a significant part of it.

vocab.definition: Not needed or required, often used to describe something that is excessive or redundant.
vocab.lyric_snippet: it's fake and it's oh-so unnecessary
vocab.context: Taylor uses 'unnecessary' to express her feelings about her former partner's attempts to stay in touch, implying that it is insincere and not needed.

songs.title: evermore
songs.lyrics:
Gray November
I've been down since July

Put me in a bad light

Evermore

This pain wouldn't be for

vocab.definition: not firmly fixed or attached, feeling a lack of direction or stability
vocab.lyric_snippet: Guess I'm feeling unmoored
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to convey a sense of disorientation and confusion, as if she's lost her sense of purpose or direction.

vocab.definition: unusual or unexpected, often in a way that is interesting or attractive
vocab.lyric_snippet: I had a feeling so peculiar
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe a unique and unsettling emotion that she's experiencing, emphasizing its unusual nature.

vocab.definition: deceived or betrayed, often in a way that is deliberate or intentional
vocab.lyric_snippet: Sending signals To be double-crossed
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to suggest that she's been misled or deceived, possibly by someone she trusted.

vocab.definition: most extreme or uncontrolled, often in a way that is intense or chaotic
vocab.lyric_snippet: Barefoot in the wildest winter
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to emphasize the intensity and harshness of the winter season, and possibly the emotions she's experiencing.

vocab.definition: stranded or wrecked, often in a way that is dramatic or catastrophic
vocab.lyric_snippet: And when I was shipwrecked
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to convey a sense of being lost or stranded, possibly emotionally or metaphorically.

vocab.definition: thrown or moved violently, often in a way that is unpredictable or uncontrollable
vocab.lyric_snippet: I'm on waves, out being tossed
vocab.context: Taylor uses this word to describe the turbulent and unpredictable nature of her emotions or circumstances.
